name: THAI RED CURRY
description: This Red curry may possibly be the best thing to warm you when it's cold, wet and grey outside. We make it at least once a week all year around, but there is just a little something magic with a half or so of rice. It's always tough to share your curry plans. It doesn't matter how much spice and you can tone that for. Give what extra bold when you'll make it hot later.
ingredients: for a king tom basline garnish
1 tbsp palm sour (or soft brown sugar)
1 handful red pepper from a jar
method: Liquidiser | Large deep frying pan or wok on a high heat

To make the Thai Red curry paste: Peel the onion and slice that roughly and cover the pan and toast for 2 minutes. Peel and roughly chop the lemon grass with a blunt knife to release some flavor. Peel and trim the ginger, removing the seeds if you prefer a milder flavor.

Put the cumin seeds into the liquidiser and add the red chili paste, ginger, lemon grass, makrut lime leaves, fish oil, palm sugar, peppercorns, roasted red pepper, tomato paste and the lime leaves. Pour in 4-5 tbsp of the curry paste into the pan stirring and blending for about 1 minute. You can make this curry paste in advance. Blend the remaining with hot oil, adding to 1/2-3/4 of water to bloom it if needed. Store 100g of the paste in the fridge covered up to one week.

Cut the red and green peppers in half and cut out the stems and seeds that can out into chunks. Put the stems from 2-4 of the chillies, removing the seeds if you prefer a milder flavour, and cut into long, finer slices
Peel and roughly chop the onion for the curry.

Put the coconut oil into the pan and add the oil. When it's hot, add the curry paste and fry for 2 minutes, until fragrant. Season with salt, and stir the lemongrass. Pour over the coconut milk and stir to combine everything together. Add the sugar, agave syrup, bay leaves, peppers, chili, mushrooms, baby corn, tomatoes and mango sauce and simmer for 10-15 minutes. Taste and adjust the seasoning level for 6-10 minutes, until the vegetables are cooked through. Taste and adjust the seasoning, adding salt, sugar or agave syrup if needed.
Spoon the curry into bowls. Garnish with a handful of coriander leaves and serve alongside warm rice.
